title: bidens involucrata
intro: Bidens involucrata, also known as beggar's lice, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive hooked seeds that cling to clothing. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Bidens involucrata include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for minor cuts and scrapes. Bidens involucrata can be prepared as a topical salve or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
